summary: Backend async job pattern is fully working end-to-end (POST /api/blueprint/jobs returns instantly, polling shows pending -> running -> done in ~75s, blueprint fetch and PDF work). However the Create.jsx frontend fix was NOT actually applied to the submit handler. The imports were updated to startBlueprintJob/getBlueprintJob but the submit() function still calls `createBlueprint(form)` which is undefined, and references an undefined `progress` variable. This throws a ReferenceError on submit, the catch shows only a generic 'Generation failed. Try again.' toast, and the user is stuck on /create with no navigation. Confirmed via ESLint (2 no-undef errors) and Playwright (submit did not enter loading state, URL stayed on /create). This is the SAME blocker as iteration_1 — the async job pattern was implemented on backend but not wired into the frontend submit.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: createBlueprint is not defined. Frontend still uses old sync helper name — never calls startBlueprintJob/getBlueprintJob. Also references undefined `progress` variable in JSX at line 185.

integration_issues.issue: Submit throws ReferenceError so no /api/blueprint/jobs POST is ever made; user sees generic error toast and stays on /create. Backend job endpoint verified working (75s completion) via curl and pytest.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Create.jsx submit(): replace `const record = await createBlueprint(form)` with the async job pattern — call `startBlueprintJob(form)` to get `{ id: jobId }`, then poll `getBlueprintJob(jobId)` every 2.5s (max 180s) until `status === 'done'`, then `navigate("/results/" + job.blueprint_id)`. Handle status==='error' with toast. Update elapsed-seconds counter into a `progress` state variable used at line 185 (currently undefined).
Consider disabling `Generation typically takes 20-60 seconds.` copy since actual generation is ~70-85s; keep language honest (e.g., 'up to ~90 seconds').
critical_code_review_comments: Create.jsx has 2 ESLint no-undef errors (createBlueprint, progress) — the review request claimed the async pattern was implemented but only imports/api.js was updated; submit() was not refactored.
api.js axios timeout is 30000ms — for a synchronous fallback that would break; not an issue for the (correct) async job polling path since each poll is fast.
Old sync endpoint /api/blueprint/generate is retained for API completeness; fine, but consider a route-level warning log so we notice if UI regresses back to it.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_blueprint_api.py (new regression test file, 6 tests all passing)

success_rate.frontend: 0% for the Create->Results integration flow (blocked by ReferenceError)

test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: One TEST_PytestCo blueprint created via lifecycle test; low-risk residue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend async job flow is fully green (pytest file: /app/backend/tests/test_blueprint_api.py, ~90s per lifecycle run). Only the Create.jsx submit() function needs fixing to complete E2E. Once fixed, re-run the Playwright flow: navigate to /create, click [data-testid=example-0] to autofill, click [data-testid=submit-blueprint], then poll page.url for /results/{id} with up to 180s timeout. Verify all 9 [data-testid^=section-] elements render and [data-testid=download-pdf] works.
rca of the issue: Root cause: incomplete refactor of Create.jsx. The api.js helpers and imports were updated to startBlueprintJob/getBlueprintJob, and progress-label testid was added, but the submit() function body was left calling the removed createBlueprint(form) helper and a `progress` variable that is never declared as state. Result: on submit, JS throws ReferenceError, caught by the generic catch which shows 'Generation failed. Try again.' and setLoading(false) — user never sees loading state and never gets redirected. Reproduction: open /create in the preview URL, click example-0 to autofill, click Generate blueprint — nothing happens (URL stays /create). Mitigation: implement the polling logic in submit() as described in action_items #1.
